description: Quiz testing comprehension of 'Huck Saves The Life Of Widow Douglas' by Mark Twain from Class 6 Roots and Wings

questions.question: Who is the author of 'The Adventures of Tom Sawyer' from which this chapter is taken?
options: Mark Twain
Charles Dickens
Jane Austen
Leo Tolstoy
questions.explanation: The content clearly states that Mark Twain (real name: Samuel Langhorne Clemens) is the author of 'The Adventures of Tom Sawyer' (1876), and this chapter is an excerpt from that novel.

questions.question: Why does Injun Joe (Red Joe) want to take revenge on Widow Douglas?
options: She stole his treasure box
Her husband sent him to prison and he cannot harm the husband because he is dead
She witnessed a murder in the graveyard
She refused to help him escape from the inn
questions.correctAnswer: Her husband sent him to prison and he cannot harm the husband because he is dead
questions.explanation: Red Joe explicitly states that the Widow's husband sent him to prison, and since Mr. Douglas had already died, Red Joe plans to take revenge on the Widow instead.

questions.question: What does Huck observe when he is hiding beside the inn at 11 o'clock at night?
options: Tom Sawyer trying to break into the inn
The Widow Douglas leaving the inn with treasure
Two men coming out with a heavy box
Red Joe arguing with the innkeeper
questions.correctAnswer: Two men coming out with a heavy box
questions.explanation: The content describes how Huck hears a faint sound, and then two men come out and walk past him, with one of them carrying a heavy box, which Huck assumes contains treasure.

questions.question: Why is the moonless night beneficial to Huck as he follows the two men?
options: It allows him to see the men more clearly
The darkness prevents him from being seen by the men he is following
It gives him the courage to follow dangerous criminals
It helps him find his way back to the town easily
questions.correctAnswer: The darkness prevents him from being seen by the men he is following
questions.explanation: The content explains that on a moonless night with clouds, the ground is very dark, which allows Huck to follow the men closely without being noticed or seen.

questions.question: Mark Twain is often called 'the Father of American Literature.'
options: True
False
questions.explanation: The content explicitly states that Mark Twain 'is widely regarded as one of the greatest American writers and is often called "the Father of American Literature."'

questions.question: Huck decided to go and tell Tom Sawyer about the two men before following them.
options: True
False
questions.explanation: The content clearly states that Huck thinks 'I do not have time to go and tell Tom. He thinks: I will have to follow them alone,' meaning he did not go to tell Tom before following the men.
